data.title: Sensex slips nearly 600 pts, Nifty below 24,950: Weak Axis Bank Q1 earnings among key factors behind market decline
data.description:
Sensex, Nifty see profit-booking in July 18 trading session.

The benchmark equity indices declined sharply on Friday, dragged by weak earnings from Axis Bank, continued foreign fund outflows and negative global cues.

Sensex tumbled 550.32 points to 81,708.92, while the Nifty declined 135.45 points lower at 24,975.20.

Traders said the broader market mood turned cautious after Axis Bank posted disappointing June quarter results, with selling pressure seen across banking and IT stocks.

Axis Bank Earnings Disappoint
Shares of Axis Bank plunged over 6 per cent after the lender reported a 3 per cent drop in consolidated net profit at Rs 6,243.72 crore in the June quarter. The results were impacted by a change in its classification of non-performing assets and loan upgrade policy.

"Axis Bank’s numbers missed expectations. Its GDR fell 4.8 per cent to USD 64.30 on Thursday, reflecting deterioration in asset quality," said Devarsh Vakil, Head of Prime Research, HDFC Securities.

The weak performance dragged down the Nifty Bank index by over 1 per cent, with 11 of its 12 constituents ending in the red.

Disappointing earnings from large-cap IT firms such as TCS and HCLTech also dampened market sentiment.

FII Selling Continues
Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) offloaded shares worth Rs 3,694.31 crore on Thursday, extending the selling trend seen through July.

"India has underperformed global peers so far this month. The selling by FIIs is a major factor. They were sellers in the first quarter, turned buyers in the next, but have resumed selling in July," said VK Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist, Geojit Financial Services.

Global Cues and Volatility Index
Weak trends in Asian equities also weighed on Indian markets. Key indices in Japan and South Korea ended in the red, mirroring concerns over global economic recovery.

Meanwhile, the India VIX, a measure of market volatility, rose nearly 4 per cent to 11.62, indicating rising nervousness among investors.

US Economic Data Dents Rate-Cut Hopes
Stronger-than-expected macro data from the US further weakened hopes of an early interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve.

Initial jobless claims dropped by 7,000 to 221,000, while retail sales rose 0.6 per cent in June, signalling continued consumer demand. The latest inflation print also showed pricing pressures remain.

Technical View
Anand James, Chief Market Strategist at Geojit Financial Services, said the Nifty's slide extended to the 25,120–25,090 region, a key support zone.

“A direct fall below 24,920 could open the door for further downsides. On the upside, the index may face resistance around 25,150–25,265,” James noted.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Markets are in Ajit’s ‘Liquid Oxygen’ state: Kotak's Pratik Gupta
data.description:
Pratik says the key catalyst is expected to be a sharp government capex push in H2, likely boosting investment and economic growth.

Equity markets are caught in a tight band—unable to rise meaningfully or fall sharply—much like Bollywood villain Ajit’s famous "liquid oxygen" trap, said Pratik Gupta, Co-head and CEO at Kotak Institutional Equities, in an interview with CNBC-TV18 this morning.

Agarwal recalled Ajit’s classic cult dialogue: “Mona Darling, isko liquid oxygen mein daal do. Liquid isko jeene nahin dega, oxygen isko marne nahin dega” (Put him in liquid oxygen. The liquid won’t let him live, the oxygen won’t let him die), to illustrate the market’s current dilemma. Tepid earnings growth and a steady supply of new equity paper are capping the upside, while strong domestic flows continue to cushion the downside, he said.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

While near-term sentiment remains cautious, there is still hope pinned on a recovery in earnings during the second half of the financial year, he added.

At present, market valuations remain elevated, with the Nifty trading at around 22 times forward earnings, levels that are considered expensive by historical standards. Earnings growth has been tepid across key sectors such as IT, consumer goods, and banking, all of which are expected to post single-digit growth for the full year.

Also read: Fund Manager Survey: Sentiment turns most bullish since 2024; India slips to fourth rank in Asia, according to BoFA

Despite the weak first-half performance on the cards, Pratik expects earnings momentum to pick up meaningfully in the second half, supported by festive demand, margin tailwinds from easing input costs, and an uptick in government-led capital expenditure. Overall, full-year earnings growth is still projected at around 11 percent.

A few key triggers could help support sentiment and improve the outlook, including a potential trade deal with the US, a reduction in retail-level petrol and diesel prices, and tweaks to the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime. However, the most meaningful catalyst is expected to be a significant government capex push in the second half of the year, which could revive investment and support broader economic growth.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Fund Manager Survey: Sentiment turns most bullish since 2024; India slips to fourth rank in Asia, according to BoFA
data.description:
Virtually no one is calling for a lower level for Asia ex-Japan equities a year out, the survey noted.

Investor optimism across Asia has surged to its highest level since December 2024, even as India’s relative appeal among global fund managers has slipped. According to Bank of America Securities’ latest Asia Fund Manager Survey, “70% of respondents now view the impact of global shocks as only slightly negative for Asia”, underscoring a broad-based recovery in risk sentiment.

The improvement is being driven by expectations of a synchronized global monetary easing cycle and hopes of a U.S.-China trade détente. “85% of the 34 major central banks are cutting rates,” the report noted, fuelling optimism for a recovery.

The share of investors anticipating a weaker global economy has dropped to a net 31%, down from 82% in April, while expectations for a weaker Asian economy have eased to 26%, a sharp improvement from 89% at the lows in April.

Yet, in terms of country allocation, India has slipped to the fourth spot, now ranking behind Japan, Taiwan, and South Korea. Taiwan and Korea are riding the momentum of the resurgent semiconductor cycle, while Korea is also benefiting from “hopes surrounding its new leadership’s policy reforms.”

Meanwhile, China’s macro outlook appears to be finding a steady state. “After significant progress in the prior two months, China’s growth outlook appears to be stabilizing at a moderate level,” BofA said. That said, “a net 10% of managers still expect a weaker Chinese economy,” unchanged from last month. Household risk appetite also remains tepid, with the survey noting that consumers are “unlikely to mobilize savings into discretionary spending and investing.”

In terms of sector preferences, fund managers are showing a clear tilt toward growth. Asia ex-Japan portfolios remain “overweight semiconductors, software, tech hardware, and banks,” while materials, energy, industrials, and real estate are underweighted. Within Japan, banks continue to dominate allocations, followed by semiconductors. “Investors prefer a value tilt over growth but do not see a definite market-cap size segment that will outperform.”

India, while still drawing attention for consumption and infrastructure plays, is seeing cooling sentiment in technology. “IT services is out of favor, mirroring our India IT Services Indicator’s plunge to a 20-month low last month.”

Fund managers are also betting on an earnings-led recovery across Asia. “Virtually no one is calling for a lower level for Asia ex-Japan equities a year out,” the survey said. A net 13% now expect an earnings pickup, marking a sharp reversal from April’s 78% who had predicted an earnings slowdown. Importantly, “consensus earnings estimates are not seen as stretched, creating scope for upgrades in the future.”

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Route Mobile shares plunge 4% as Q1 net profit, revenue takes a hit
data.description:
Route Mobile shares are down 30 percent since the beginning of the year.

Shares of Route Mobile tumbled as much as 4 percent to Rs 968 in morning trade on July 18 after the company reported a weak set of numbers for the first quarter of FY26.

Route Mobile reported a 32.23 percent year-on-year decline in net profit to Rs 53.21 crore for the quarter ended June 2025, compared to Rs 78.52 crore in the same period last year.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

Route Mobile reported a subdued performance for the June quarter, with revenue declining 10.6 percent sequentially and 4.8 percent year-on-year to Rs 1,050.8 crore. The dip was largely due to a slowdown in international business and the discontinuation of certain low-margin customer contracts.

EBITDA margin narrowed to 8.9 percent from 10.4 percent in the previous quarter, while profit after tax fell 6 percent quarter-on-quarter and 32 percent compared to last year.

Also read: India a 'standout investment destination' amid global volatility: KKR

In a key management change, CEO Gautam Badalia has stepped down with effect from July 17, 2025. The Board has approved the re-designation of Rajdipkumar Gupta as Managing Director and CEO, effective July 18, 2025.

Amid shifting global dynamics and increased caution among enterprise clients, Route Mobile plans to shift its focus to cost optimisation and automation through AI.

In an nteraction with CNBC-TV18 in May, Badalia had said the company was committed to driving stronger profitability through efficiency gains. He also underlined Route’s strategic advantage of having direct connectivity with over 450 mobile network operators globally—a critical edge as clients increasingly prefer working directly with trusted providers over intermediaries.

At about 10 am, shares of the company were trading at Rs 972, lower by 3.6 percent from the last close on the NSE. Route Mobile shares are down 30 percent since the beginning of the year.

data.title: Godrej Properties shares gain for the 5th day on acquiring 48-acre land parcel in Bengaluru
data.description:
Godrej Properties climbs on Bengaluru deal

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Godrej Properties extended their winning streak to a fifth consecutive session, rising to Rs 2,358 apiece on July 18, after the company announced the acquisition of a 48-acre land parcel in Bengaluru, Karnataka.

Over the past five trading days, the stock of this real estate company has gained 6 percent, compared with a 0.5 percent decline in the benchmark Nifty 50 index.

The company said it acquired the 48-acre land parcel in the Doddaballapur micro-market of North Bengaluru. Godrej Properties added that the site is located near the Satellite Town Ring Road (STRR). The development on this land will primarily consist of plotted units, with an estimated development potential of around 1.1 million square feet.

This acquisition follows the company’s announcement earlier this week of a 50-acre residential land deal in Raipur.

The Raipur property, situated close to central Raipur, the railway station, Swami Vivekananda Airport, and the upcoming Raipur–Hyderabad and Raipur–Visakhapatnam expressways, will be developed into a premium plotted residential project with an estimated saleable area of approximately 9.5 lakh square feet.

Last month, Godrej Properties reported sales worth over Rs 2,000 crore during the launch of the first phase of Barca at Godrej MSR City in Devanahalli, Bengaluru.

Also last month, the company announced another acquisition in Pune, acquiring 16 acres in the Upper Kharadi locality. This project, located near IT hubs such as Viman Nagar, Magarpatta, and Hadapsar, is expected to generate revenue of around Rs 3,100 crore.

data.title: LTIMindtree shares fall 2% after in-line Q1 results, brokerages share mixed stance
data.description:
LTIMindtree Q1 steady, brokerages cautious

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of LTIMindtree fell over 2 percent to Rs 5,078 apiece on July 18 after the company posted an in-line set of results for the April-June quarter (Q1FY26). While brokerages maintained their ratings on the stock following the Q1 numbers, they indicated limited upside from current levels.

Morgan Stanley, which has an "Equal-weight" rating with a target price of Rs 5,400, noted that the management expects to sustain both growth and margin momentum. However, the brokerage felt that the commentary could have been more bullish if macroeconomic conditions were more supportive.

Morgan Stanley added that LTIMindtree’s limited earnings per share (EPS) changes compared to peers is a positive sign.

Catch all the market action on our LIVE blog

Global brokerage HSBC maintained its "Buy" rating and raised the target price to Rs 6,000 from Rs 5,900 earlier. HSBC said the Q1 performance was broadly in line with expectations and reflected continued improvement in the company’s fundamentals.

Over the medium to long term, it expects LTIMindtree’s growth to be nearly double that of mega-cap IT companies, with scope for strong growth acceleration at still-attractive valuations.

Nuvama also reiterated its "Buy" rating and sharply increased its target price to Rs 6,200 from Rs 5,200. The brokerage believes LTIMindtree occupies a unique position — trading below the expensive, high-growth Tier-2 IT firms and above the cheaper, low-growth Tier-1 firms.

According to Nuvama, this places the company in a sweet spot for “growth at a reasonable valuation” with a favourable risk-reward profile.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

On the other hand, Nomura maintained a "Neutral" rating with a target price of Rs 5,040. It highlighted improving growth visibility but added that FY26 is likely to be a “year of repair” and accounted for a slightly higher tax rate in its forecasts.

For the June quarter, LTIMindtree reported a 10.6 percent rise in net profit to Rs 1,254.1 crore. Revenue from operations increased 7.6 percent to Rs 9,840.6 crore, compared to Rs 9,142.6 crore in the same quarter of FY25.

data.title: Polycab India shares rise nearly 3% on strong Q1; UBS ups target price
data.description:
Polycab India shares have risen 14 percent in the last month but still trade 5 percent lower on a year-to-date basis.

Shares of Polycab India rallied nearly 3 percent to Rs 7,065 per share in morning trade on July 18 after Citi Research and UBS dished out bullish calls on the stock following its first quarter earnings.

Net profit for the quarter increased by 50 percent from the year-ago quarter to Rs 600 crore, while revenue from operations for the quarter increased by 25.7 percent to Rs 5,906 crore.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

Polycab's Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation and Amortisation (EBITDA) increased by 47.1 percent to Rs 858 crore. EBITDA margins for the quarter expanded by over 200 basis points from the year-ago period to 14.5 percent.

Following the development, Citi Research maintained a Buy rating with a target price of Rs 7,700 per share, implying an upside potential of 12 percent from the last close on the NSE. The wires and cables segment continued to lead growth, with the FMEG (fast-moving electrical goods) division also showing strength, posting 18 percent revenue growth and a 283 bps expansion in margin to 2.1 percent. Notably, the FMEG business turned in its second consecutive profitable quarter.

Also read: Trading Plan: Can Nifty 50, Bank Nifty manage to rebound amid rangebound trading?

UBS also reiterated a Buy rating on Polycab, raising its target price to Rs 8,100 per share. The new target forecasts an upside of 18 percent. The brokerage highlighted a robust start to the fiscal year and suggested that both the cables and wires (C&W) and FMEG segments delivered strong performances.

Polycab’s wires and cables segment posted a robust performance, with revenue rising to Rs 5,228 crore in the June quarter, up from Rs 3,995 crore a year ago. The FMEG (fast-moving electrical goods) business also saw healthy growth, with revenue increasing to Rs 454 crore from Rs 385 crore in the same period last year. Meanwhile, revenue from the EPC (engineering, procurement, and construction) segment declined to Rs 347 crore from Rs 427 crore a year ago.

Notably, the EPC division, previously clubbed under the ‘others’ segment, will now be reported separately after meeting the qualitative criteria for distinct disclosure.

At about 9:40 am, shares of the company were trading at Rs 7,050, higher by 2.5 percent from the last close on the NSE. Polycab India shares have risen 14 percent in the last month but still trade 5 percent lower on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Sensex down 200 pts, Nifty below 25,100; Axis Bank slips 4%, Wipro up 3% on Q1 beat
data.description:
Laggards on the index included Axis Bank, Bharti Airtel, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Grasim, and SBI Life Insurance.

Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex opened lower on July 18, setting the stage for a weak end to the week. This came despite strong global cues, as US markets rallied on upbeat retail sales data and a decline in weekly jobless claims.

At about 9:25 am, the Sensex was down 209.27 points or 0.25 percent at 82,049.97, and the Nifty was down 46.90 points or 0.19 percent at 25,064.55. About 1664 shares advanced, 1020 shares declined, and 142 shares unchanged.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

"Global uncertainty and a muted start to the earnings season are weighing on investor sentiment. However, sustained liquidity inflows are helping to cushion the downside. This is clearly evident in the current market environment, where a divergent trend is visible—while the benchmark index is under pressure, mainly due to weakness in heavyweight IT stock," Ajit Mishra, Senior Vice President at Religare Broking, said. "At the same time, rate-sensitive sectors like auto, realty, and select banking, along with continued interest in defensives like FMCG and pharma, are not only limiting the losses but also offering ample long-side trading opportunities," he added.

As for individual stocks, Wipro gained over 3 percent after the company reported a June quarter performance that was better than expected, driven by strong execution of large deals. However, brokerages remain in a wait-and-watch mode as the management has guided for a stronger recovery in the second half of this fiscal year.

Also read: India a 'standout investment destination' amid global volatility: KKR

Axis Bank shares slid 4 percent after lender posted a weak set of numbers for the June quarter. Profit and net interest income (NII) growth were muted, fresh slippages increased sharply owing to a technical impact, and margins contracted. Brokerages expressed disappointment over the Q1 performance and downgraded their ratings on the stock. The Global Depositary Receipts (GDRs) of Axis Bank also tumbled 5 percent to $64.3 on July 18 after the bank's asset quality worsened during the June quarter.

Shares of Polycab India rallied 2 percent after Citi Research and UBS dished out bullish calls on the stock following its first quarter earnings. Net profit for the quarter increased by 50 percent from the year-ago quarter to Rs 600 crore, while revenue from operations for the quarter increased by 25.7 percent to Rs 5,906 crore.

From a technical perspective, the psychological support of 25,000 remains intact, while the 25,350 mark continues to act as a formidable ceiling. Until the index breaks out from this congestion zone, significant directional moves are unlikely to unfold. From a technical standpoint, Nifty continues to trade below its 10-day and 20-day exponential moving averages, both of which are near the 25,250 mark. This highlights the persistent selling pressure near the upper boundary of the current range.

Read more: Perplexity deal gives Airtel dual win: AI branding and monetization potential, say analysts

Tata Steel, JSW Steel, Hero MotoCorp, ONGC, and Hindalco were the top gainers on the Nifty. Laggards on the index included Axis Bank, Bharti Airtel, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Grasim, and SBI Life Insurance.
